outputs.title: Graduate futures evidence pack and responsible-use model
outputs.description: The data specification, interpretation principles, caveat handling and visual or narrative pack model that help programme teams understand their students' graduate futures evidence responsibly.
acceptanceCriteria: Data specification, caveats, taxonomy and comparator principles are coherent across the first and second pack versions.
The first evidence pack has been tested with selected users by September 2026.
The programme-facing pack can be understood by academic colleagues without appearing to rank or judge programmes.

outputs.title: Guided programme conversation and self-review model
outputs.description: The model for helping programme teams move from story, to evidence, to questions, to action, including prompts, facilitation notes, responsible-use guidance, early programme partner testing and a self-review route.
acceptanceCriteria: Conversation materials guide colleagues from programme story and evidence to practical reflection and action.
Early programme partners have tested the framing, usability, outputs and tone of the approach.
The model distinguishes supported review and self-review routes for later programme engagement.

outputs.title: Programme Graduate Futures Profile and action set
outputs.description: The repeatable output set that programmes, departments or agreed groups can produce through supported review or self-review: an evidence-informed graduate futures profile, practical actions, story material and light reflections.
acceptanceCriteria: Agreed programme groups produce evidence-informed profiles, practical actions and story material where appropriate by September 2027.
Every agreed programme either completes a supported review or is enabled to complete a self-review by September 2028.
Outputs distinguish evidence, interpretation, action and student-facing story material.

outputs.title: Living evidence resource and institutional handoff model
outputs.description: A sustainable model for refreshing the evidence, maintaining the pack, updating guidance and connecting graduate futures evidence to quality assurance and continual improvement cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Refresh cadence and owners are confirmed.
Data, visualisation, access and interpretation guidance maintenance routes are agreed.
Graduate futures evidence has a clear place in quality assurance, continual improvement and programme enhancement cycles.

outputs.title: Institutional graduate futures synthesis and feed-through
outputs.description: Institutional synthesis of programme stories, destination patterns, strengths, recurring questions, support needs and implications for related King's Edge work.
acceptanceCriteria: Faculty and programme learning is synthesised into strengths, questions, support needs and institutional implications.
Findings feed related King's Edge deliverables and curriculum enhancement activity.
The synthesis avoids ranking programmes and distinguishes evidence, interpretation and recommended institutional action.

measures.title: Evidence specification readiness
measures.questionAnswered: Has the evidence specification been completed in time for the first pack?
measures.measure: Completion of data specification, inventory, caveat register, taxonomy, comparator principles and responsible-use approach.
measures.target: Evidence specification and interpretation framework complete by September 2026.

measures.title: First evidence pack validation
measures.questionAnswered: Can selected users understand and use the first pack to interpret programme graduate futures evidence responsibly?
measures.measure: Testing findings covering clarity, credibility, responsible interpretation, caveats, usefulness and ability to support programme story.
measures.target: First evidence pack tested with selected users by September 2026.

measures.title: Programme pack sharing and learning from use
measures.questionAnswered: Have the first programme-facing packs been shared in a way that helps colleagues recognise strengths, ask useful questions and understand caveats?
measures.measure: Completion of programme-facing packs, short user guide, sharing route and captured learning from early use.
measures.target: Programme-facing packs shared and learning captured by Christmas 2026.

measures.title: Early institutional learning
measures.questionAnswered: Has early use of the packs created useful faculty and institutional learning without treating programme packs as final judgements?
measures.measure: Faculty conversations completed and early institutional summary submitted to the agreed senior education forum.
measures.target: Faculty conversations held and early institutional summary submitted by Christmas 2026.

measures.title: Early programme partner testing
measures.questionAnswered: Have early programme partners shown that the pack and guided conversation help surface programme value as well as areas for enhancement?
measures.measure: Early programme conversations and feedback on usability, credibility, framing, story value, actionability and outputs.
measures.target: Early programme partners complete meaningful tests before wider engagement.

measures.title: Programme story and responsible interpretation quality
measures.questionAnswered: Are colleagues using the evidence to tell accurate, context-sensitive programme stories and interpret caveats responsibly?
measures.measure: Review of early profiles, reflection templates, caveat use, facilitator feedback and programme confirmation of story material.
measures.cadence: During testing and programme engagement reviews
measures.target: Programme story material is evidence-linked, context-sensitive and agreed with programme teams.

measures.title: Shared prioritisation and focused engagement
measures.questionAnswered: Has the next phase reached agreed programme groups through a fair shared prioritisation process?
measures.measure: Agreed prioritisation criteria, completed supported conversations, reflections, practical actions and captured faculty learning.
measures.target: Agreed programme groups complete supported conversations by September 2027.

measures.title: Programme profile and action quality
measures.questionAnswered: Does the process help programmes produce credible graduate futures profiles and realistic actions?
measures.measure: Quality of Programme Graduate Futures Profiles, practical action sets, story material and identified support needs.
measures.target: Programmes engaged through supported review or self-review produce proportionate evidence-informed outputs.

measures.title: Living resource and institutional handoff
measures.questionAnswered: Has the evidence pack become a maintained resource connected to quality assurance and continual improvement?
measures.measure: Confirmed owner, refresh cadence, access route, interpretation guidance maintenance and quality assurance or continual improvement handoff.
measures.target: Living resource ownership and institutional handoff confirmed before full programme engagement completes.

measures.title: Full programme review or enabled self-review and synthesis
measures.questionAnswered: Has every agreed programme completed supported review or been enabled to self-review, and has institutional learning been synthesised?
measures.measure: Coverage confirmation, self-review enablement evidence and synthesis of strengths, recurring questions, support needs and implications.
measures.target: Every agreed programme has completed supported review or enabled self-review, with synthesis completed during 2027/28.

resources.fundingSummary: Whole-route resourcing depends on protected data and visualisation capacity, a modest summer student placement for early data and visualisation work, delivery coordination, faculty facilitator time, King's Academy input and a sustainable ownership route for the living evidence resource. Step-level resources describe where each type of capacity is needed in the delivery route.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own or co-own the graduate futures evidence resource, interpretation principles, caveat guidance and ongoing refresh route.
Lead the evidence pack, guided conversation and engagement model across the deliverable.
Advise on academic development, facilitation design and proportionate programme enhancement language.
existingCapacity.type: existing academic development capacity to align

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide the faculty decision and delivery route for early partner selection, shared prioritisation, supported review and self-review.
Own benefit realisation and follow up agreed programme actions through normal department and faculty routes.
Maintain institutional graduate futures coherence and lead the synthesis into related King's Edge work.

newInvestment.rationale: Needed to support data preparation, visualisation development, pack production and early user testing across Steps 1 and 2.

newInvestment.item: Protected analytical and data visualisation capacity
newInvestment.rationale: Needed to produce, validate and maintain a credible evidence pack and scalable visualisation route rather than relying on ad hoc manual production.
Could support pack preparation, programme engagement logistics, self-review tracking, evidence handling and institutional synthesis.
Needed to avoid the evidence pack, guidance and visualisation route becoming a one-off project output.

enablingConditions.condition: Sustained access to graduate outcomes, LEO, TEF, benchmarking, enrolment supplement, aspiration and relevant internal datasets.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The model cannot provide a credible programme-level graduate futures view.

enablingConditions.condition: Power BI prioritisation or equivalent scalable data visualisation route.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The evidence pack may remain static or manually produced, limiting institutional use.

enablingConditions.condition: Faculty agreement to own local facilitation and self-review support rather than expecting central delivery for every programme.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The central team becomes the bottleneck and full programme engagement is not achievable.

enablingConditions.condition: Heads of Department accept benefit ownership for programme reflections and actions.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Programme reflections and actions are produced but not owned or followed up.

enablingConditions.condition: Programme confirmation is accepted as sufficient approval for student-facing or applicant-facing story material.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Story activation becomes too slow, over-governed or disconnected from academic ownership.

enablingConditions.condition: Quality assurance and continual improvement routes accept graduate futures evidence as part of ongoing programme reflection.
enablingConditions.owner: Quality assurance and continual improvement owners
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pack remains available but does not shape sustained programme enhancement.

investmentAsk.priority: High for September 2026 evidence pack testing, Christmas 2026 programme pack sharing and the shared Step 1 and Step 2 summer placement
investmentAsk.rationale: The deliverable needs protected analytical, visualisation and coordination capacity, plus a modest student placement of approximately £3,000 including on-costs to support early data and visualisation work. Step-level plans identify where the capacity is used; the deliverable-level ask is the overall resourcing decision.

risks.title: The work becomes too centrally resource-intensive
risks.description: Central teams cannot facilitate programme conversations for every programme or manually sustain all pack production.
risks.mitigation: Use faculty-owned facilitation, supported self-review and a common toolkit, with central support focused on evidence, method, guidance and synthesis.

risks.title: Data is over-interpreted or used punitively
risks.description: Small numbers, suppression, volatility, coding issues or comparator misuse could lead to unfair or unhelpful interpretations.
risks.mitigation: Use caveat guidance, responsible-use principles, facilitation support and clear language throughout the evidence pack and conversation route.

risks.title: Programme story becomes unsupported marketing language
risks.description: Student-facing or applicant-facing language could drift beyond the evidence or lose academic ownership.
risks.mitigation: Require evidence-linked proof points and programme confirmation before story material is used more visibly.

risks.title: Faculty engagement is inconsistent
risks.description: Local facilitation, supported review and self-review may vary in quality, tone and completion across faculties.
risks.mitigation: Use a common pack, guided conversation materials, responsible-use guidance, faculty ownership and progress tracking.

risks.title: Prioritisation is perceived as punitive
risks.description: Programmes included in focused engagement may feel labelled as weak or problematic.
risks.mitigation: Use a transparent shared prioritisation process that includes evidence, faculty judgement, programme context, strengths, learning value and support needs.

risks.title: The evidence resource is not sustained
risks.description: The pack, visualisation, refresh rhythm and guidance could remain dependent on project goodwill after initial delivery.
risks.mitigation: Confirm refresh cadence, owner, access route, interpretation guidance maintenance and quality assurance or continual improvement handoff before full programme engagement completes.

risks.title: Self-review becomes abandonment
risks.description: Programmes using the self-review route may receive materials but not enough guidance or support to use them well.
risks.mitigation: Provide clear guidance, responsible-use materials and a route for help where evidence is unclear or sensitive.

issues.title: Early data, visualisation and placement resource needs confirmation
issues.description: The deliverable requires a decision on protected analytical and visualisation capacity, including the approximately £3,000 summer student placement for Steps 1 and 2.

issues.title: Faculty-owned engagement route needs to be locked
issues.description: The model depends on Vice Deans Education or delegates confirming local facilitation, supported review, self-review expectations and benefit ownership routes.

issues.title: Shared prioritisation process needs agreement
issues.description: The institution needs an agreed way to combine evidence, faculty knowledge, programme context and local judgement when selecting programme groups for focused engagement.

issues.title: Living resource and institutional handoff route needs confirmation
issues.description: The evidence pack, refresh rhythm, interpretation guidance and synthesis need a confirmed ownership route connected to quality assurance and continual improvement cycles.
issues.owner: Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace

assumptions.title: Evidence can support both story and enhancement
assumptions.summary: The same evidence can help programmes tell a stronger graduate futures story and identify where students may need more visible or consistent support.

assumptions.title: Faculty-led engagement is necessary for scale
assumptions.summary: The central team cannot support every programme directly, so faculties must own supported review and self-review routes.

assumptions.title: Early programme partners will improve the approach
assumptions.summary: Early partners will help test whether the pack and guided conversation are useful, fair, proportionate and academically credible.

assumptions.title: Shared prioritisation can be agreed constructively
assumptions.summary: Programme groups for focused engagement can be agreed through evidence, faculty knowledge, programme context and local judgement.

assumptions.title: Programmes can self-review where support needs are lower
assumptions.summary: Some programmes can use the pack, guidance and conversation materials without a supported conversation, provided the self-review route is clear.

assumptions.title: Programme confirmation is enough for visible story use
assumptions.summary: Programme teams can confirm that student-facing or applicant-facing story language is accurate, fair and evidence-informed before local use.

assumptions.title: The evidence resource can become a maintained service
assumptions.summary: The deliverable assumes that visualisation maintenance, data refresh, interpretation guidance and access routes can move from project delivery into a sustainable institutional route.

decisions.title: Confirm protected resource for early evidence and visualisation work
decisions.description: Decide whether additional analytical, visualisation or coordination capacity is required, including the approximately £3,000 summer student placement to support Steps 1 and 2.
decisions.decisionMaker: Vice Deans Education or delegates, with Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Confirm first evidence pack scope and responsible-use approach
decisions.description: Agree minimum viable sources, pack structure, comparator principles, caveats, interpretation guidance and user testing standard for September 2026.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Confirm early testing and sharing route
decisions.description: Agree selected users for the first pack, how programme-facing packs will be shared, and which senior education forum receives the early institutional summary.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Vice Deans Education or delegates

decisions.title: Confirm guided programme conversation model
decisions.description: Agree the conversation structure, role of King's Academy input, facilitation route, light templates and expectations for follow-up.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Vice Deans Education or delegates

decisions.title: Confirm early programme partners
decisions.description: Agree the early programme partners that will test whether the pack and guided conversation are useful, fair and academically credible.
decisions.decisionMaker: Vice Deans Education or delegates, with Mark Peace and Daniel Robson

decisions.title: Confirm shared prioritisation process
decisions.description: Agree criteria and decision route for identifying programme groups where the evidence can be most useful, combining evidence with faculty knowledge, programme context and local judgement.

decisions.title: Confirm living evidence resource ownership and handoff
decisions.description: Agree the owner, refresh rhythm, access route, interpretation guidance maintenance and connection to quality assurance and continual improvement cycles.
decisions.decisionMaker: Careers and Employability Service with Mark Peace and relevant quality assurance or continual improvement owners

decisions.title: Confirm review coverage and self-review threshold
decisions.description: Agree the final programme population, which programmes need supported review, which can self-review, and what evidence confirms that a programme has been enabled to self-review.

decisions.title: Confirm institutional synthesis and feed-through route
decisions.description: Agree where the institutional synthesis will be reviewed and how findings feed related King's Edge deliverables, curriculum enhancement, skills, opportunity and student-facing story work.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace and Vice Deans Education or delegates
